sections.paragraph:
ἐπίστασις, εως, ἡ [ἐφίστημι]

—1. ‘condition of coming to a halt’, stopping ἐ. ποιεῖν ὄχλου cause a crowd to stop = ‘cause a public disturbance’ (a source of anxiety for those concerned about public order) Ac 24:12.

—2. ‘experience of pressure to do someth.’, urgent claim ἡ ἐ. μοι ἡ ϰαϑ’ ἡμέραν the daily urgency pressing upon me 2 Cor 11:28.
